entries.heading: Geography
entries.text:
Aethermoor is a mystical realm where magic flows through ancient ley lines, creating zones of wild power. The land is divided into several regions:

• The Crystal Highlands - Mountains rich with magical crystals
• Verdant Expanse - Lush forests inhabited by druids and spirits
• Ashen Wastes - Desert corrupted by dark magic
• Frozen North - Icy tundra home to frost giants
• The Fallen Kingdom - Ruins of an ancient civilization

entries.heading: The First Mages
entries.text: Thousands of years ago, the first mortals learned to harness the raw magical energy that permeated the world. These pioneers became known as the Archmages, and they established the Council of Seven to govern the use of magic.

entries.heading: The Sundering
entries.text:
A cataclysmic war between the Archmages shattered the ancient kingdom. One mage, consumed by ambition, attempted to claim all magic for himself. The resulting battle tore reality itself, creating the Void - a dimension of pure destruction.

The Archmages sacrificed themselves to seal the Void, but their kingdom fell to ruin. Magic became wild and unpredictable, and the knowledge of the old ways was lost.

entries.heading: Present Day
entries.text: You are among a new generation of magic users, rediscovering the lost arts. The Void still threatens reality, and whispers speak of a prophecy - a King Wizard who will reunite the fragments of ancient power and restore balance to the world.

entries.heading: Schools of Magic
entries.text:
Elemental - Fire, ice, lightning, and earth manipulation
Arcane - Pure magical energy, force fields, teleportation
Necromancy - Death magic, summoning undead, life drain
Restoration - Healing, protection, cleansing
Illusion - Mind tricks, invisibility, confusion

entries.heading: Ley Lines
entries.text: Invisible rivers of magical energy crisscross the world. Standing near a ley line nexus greatly enhances spellcasting power but also attracts dangerous magical creatures.

entries.heading: Spell Crafting
entries.text: Advanced mages can create custom spells by combining elemental essences and arcane formulas. This art was thought lost but is slowly being rediscovered.

entries.heading: The Mage's Guild
entries.text: A collection of scholars and wizards dedicated to preserving magical knowledge. They offer training, quests, and sell rare spells to members.

entries.heading: The Order of the Silver Shield
entries.text: Warriors sworn to protect the innocent from magical threats. They view magic with suspicion but recognize its necessity. Paladins and Shield Warriors often join their ranks.

entries.heading: The Shadow Covenant
entries.text: A secretive group of necromancers and dark mages. They seek forbidden knowledge and are rumored to be trying to reopen the Void.

entries.heading: The Druids of the Old Grove
entries.text: Nature guardians who maintain balance between civilization and the wild. They practice primal magic and commune with spirits.

entries.heading: Elder Sophia
entries.text: A wise oracle and quest giver. She claims to have lived through the Sundering and possesses fragments of ancient knowledge. Her prophecies have never been wrong.

entries.heading: Merchant Aldric
entries.text: A traveling merchant with connections across the realm. Some say he's merely an opportunist, others whisper he's a retired adventurer with a mysterious past.

entries.heading: Captain Marcus
entries.text: Commander of the city guard. A veteran warrior who has seen countless battles. He trusts actions more than words and respects proven strength.

entries.heading: The Nameless King
entries.text: An ancient undead ruler who haunts the Fallen Kingdom. Legend says he was the Archmage who caused the Sundering, cursed to wander eternity as punishment.

entries.heading: Common Threats
entries.text:
Goblins - Weak but numerous, often attack in packs
Bandits - Human outlaws, decent with weapons
Wolves - Fast predators, hunt at night
Slimes - Resistant to physical damage, weak to fire

entries.heading: Magical Creatures
entries.text:
Golems - Animated stone constructs, high armor
Wisps - Floating spirits, drain mana
Elementals - Embodiments of fire/ice/lightning
Dragons - Rare but devastating, legendary treasures

entries.heading: Undead
entries.text:
Skeletons - Basic undead warriors
Zombies - Slow but resilient
Ghosts - Incorporeal, immune to physical damage
Liches - Powerful undead mages, extremely dangerous
